text.title: bun-runtime Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for bun-runtime as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Running npm install after bun install, creating both package-lock.json and bun.lockb
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: package-lock.json takes precedence in some tools, causing non-deterministic dependency resolution. Pick one package manager and lock to it.

failure-mode.description: Importing node:cluster, node:vm, or process.binding() — these differ or fail under Bun
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bun's Node compat is >95% but not 100%. These specific APIs have known gaps; use Bun.spawn or single-process multi-worker alternatives.

failure-mode.description: Using child_process.exec instead of Bun's $ shell API or Bun.spawn
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bun provides $ (shell) and Bun.spawn as native alternatives with better ergonomics.

failure-mode.description: Using Bun.file() or Bun.serve() in a package consumed by both Bun and Node apps
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Node import crashes with "Bun is not defined". Restrict Bun-native APIs to entry points or dev tooling; use isomorphic alternatives in shared packages.

failure-mode.description: Writing Bun.serve for a Cloudflare Workers deploy target
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Workers run on V8 isolates, not Bun. Workers expect export default { fetch }. Use bun build --target=browser for Workers.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: --hot persists module state across reloads (DB pools, singletons). After config changes, old instances remain alive. Use --watch for dev; deploy a built bundle for prod.
